amphibian0000000.name: Amphibian
amphibian0000000.description:
Beasts which dwell within or nearby water. Amphibians are typically dexterous, but some varieties are powerful or large in size.

  • Size: variable

  • Stride: variable

apparatus0000000.name: Apparatus
apparatus0000000.description: Constructs built for simple purposes, such as multi-stage automated trap devices or machines to perform a specific repetitive function.

arachnid00000000.name: Arachnid
arachnid00000000.description:
Beasts from the arachnid family which spin webs to ensnare and prey upon small creatures or insects. Arachnids are typically dexterous and patient, but some varieties favor power or aggression.

  • Size: variable

  • Stride: variable

automaton0000000.name: Automaton
automaton0000000.description: Constructs designed as a reflection of humanoids to serve in the completion of simple labor tasks.

boar000000000000.name: Boar
boar000000000000.description: Mammals with thick hide, dangerous tusks, and a powerful low-seated center of mass.

canine0000000000.name: Hyena
canine0000000000.description: Mammals from the hyena family. Hyenas are carnivorous pack hunters with powerful necks and jaws.

drake00000000000.name: Drake
drake00000000000.description:
Serpentine creatures which are lesser kin of dragons. Drakes generally live and hunt in packs, unlike more solitary full dragons. Drakes range from younger and smaller varieties often called "Drakelings" to huge specimens sometimes referred to as "Ultra Drakes".

Observed size variations include:

  • Drakeling: Size 3, Stride 10

  • Drake: Size 6, Stride 16

  • Ultra Drake: Size 9, Stride 12

Different types of Drakes feature a different range of damage resistances and vulnerabilities depending on their sub-species.

dustDevil0000000.name: Dust Devil
dustDevil0000000.description: Elemental embodiments of swirling dust and grit. The smallest, a Dust Mote, is among the weakest of all elementals; greater specimens grow into devils, whirlwinds, and roaring dust storms.

earthElemental00.name: Earth Elemental
earthElemental00.description: Elemental creatures corresponding to the element of Earth.

fireElemental000.name: Fire Elemental
fireElemental000.description: Elemental creatures corresponding to the element of Fire.

frostElemental00.name: Frost Elemental
frostElemental00.description: Elemental creatures corresponding to the element of Frost.

ghoul00000000000.name: Ghoul
ghoul00000000000.description: Malevolent and cunning undead who retain enough of their mortal cognition to construct and execute upon plans and schemes to torment or destroy living creatures.

graveHound000000.name: Grave Hound
graveHound000000.description: Terrible undead canines who have fallen to corruption and become servants of undeath. Grave Hounds are found as guardians of unholy places, companions to the forces of undeath, or as terrible packs dispatched to hunt and terrorize the living.

hobgoblin0000000.name: Hobgoblin
hobgoblin0000000.description: Hobgoblins are humanoids which typically congregate in martial societies which are orderly and disciplined. Hobgoblins are both cunning and uncompromising, making dangerous foes or powerful allies.

mudElemental0000.name: Mud Elemental
mudElemental0000.description: Shapeless elementals of caustic mud and sludge: soft, mindless, and corrosive. They engulf and dissolve what they cannot crush.

ogre000000000000.name: Ogre
ogre000000000000.description: Hulking giantkin which are reknowned for their brute strength and direct problem-solving approach.

rodent0000000000.name: Rodent
rodent0000000000.description: Beasts which typically congregate in high populations in and around humanoid settlements and are opportunistic scavengers.

skeleton00000000.name: Skeleton
skeleton00000000.description: Malevolent undying creatures brought to life by necromancy, created from the skeletal remains of long-dead humanoids. Driven by unholy forces beyond their control, @ref[name] rarely exist of their own accord, and are often encountered as the minions of evil spellcasters and greater undead.

stoneElemental00.name: Stone Elemental
stoneElemental00.description: Towering elementals of solid living rock: ponderous, immovable, and enduring. They channel the raw might of the earth through their own immense mass.

stormElemental00.name: Storm Elemental
stormElemental00.description: Elemental creatures corresponding to the element of Storm.

taxCrystalSpider.name: Crystal Spider
taxCrystalSpider.description: Arachnid comprised of or encrusted in refractive crystalline plates and formations.

taxonomySlime000.name: Slime
taxonomySlime000.description: Creatures made primarily of gelatinous material. They are generally slow, small in size, and incapable of spellcasting or speech. Some exceptions have been documented with variations of slimes which are large, fast, or capable of communication.

taxonomyWarMachi.name: War Machine
taxonomyWarMachi.description: Constructs built for the purpose of being instruments of war or formidable guardians.

werewolf00000000.name: Werewolf
werewolf00000000.description: Lycanthropic shapeshifters who walk as men but take wolven form.

wingedTerror0000.name: Winged Terror
wingedTerror0000.description: Monstrous creatures that rule the skies and prey upon those bel0w.

wraith0000000000.name: Wraith
wraith0000000000.description: Wraiths are incorporeal undead born from souls that refused release - their physical form dissolved entirely into shadow, leaving only spite and sorrow given motion. They ignore conventional defenses, their touch bypassing armor and parry alike to assault the psychic and necrotic vulnerabilities of the living directly. Prolonged exposure to a wraith's presence drains morale as much as health, and those who survive an encounter often find their focus and resolve slow to return.

zombie0000000000.name: Zombie
zombie0000000000.description: The shambling corpses of the restless dead, their still-rotting bodies bloated with putrescence. They move without regard for the wounds they sustain and seek only mindless consumption.
